steps.title: Identify the Opposing Views
steps.content: Text 1 advocates for organizing Soyinka's works into three chronological phases (“it is best...to understand Soyinka's body of work...chronologically”), while Text 2 challenges this method, arguing that it “overlook[s] potential commonalities...that span across these phases” and may “discount significant differences...written around the same time.”

steps.title: Match the Opposition to the Choices
steps.content: Choice A directly captures this difference: Text 1 finds the phase approach useful, while Text 2 argues it's limiting by overlooking cross-phase connections and within-period variations. The other choices either misrepresent what the authors claim or introduce entirely unsupported content. That matches choice A — A is correct.
